title: Scaling intra-urban climate fluctuations

data_scope.time_window: daily temperature products; exact per-product date ranges in Methods (not stated in abstract)

core_conclusion: Across 142 cities worldwide, the full marginal and joint probability distributions of intra-urban temperature and PM air pollution collapse onto a small set of universal scaling functions inspired by finite-size scaling in statistical physics. A logarithmic relation links street-network properties to these climate fields, so average network features alone characterize the full variability of heat and pollution within and across cities, enabling climate information to enter reduced-complexity urban models.
why_it_matters: Shifts urban scaling from boundary-sensitive averages to whole distributions; imports finite-size scaling from physics to collapse heterogeneous cities onto master curves; makes street-network morphology an interpretable predictor of heat and air quality; and lets climate fields feed fast reduced-complexity planning models.
why_nature: Universal, transferable law across 142 cities; cross-disciplinary mechanism (statistical physics applied to urban climate); couples human street structure to environmental exposure; policy- and model-translatable with fully open data and code.
added_value_to_my_research: Whole-network signature of climate via explainable ML ranking of street-graph descriptors
